questions.q: Who journeyed to plead with Rama to take back the throne of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Bharata, Rama's younger brother, traveled to the forest to beg Rama to return and rule.

questions.q: To which forest hermitage did Bharata travel to meet Rama?
questions.explanation: Rama was living at Chitrakoot during his early exile when Bharata came to find him.

questions.q: What did Bharata want Rama to do?
options: Take back the crown and rule Ayodhya
questions.explanation: Bharata begged Rama to return to Ayodhya and accept the kingship that was rightfully his.

questions.q: Did Rama agree to return to Ayodhya at Bharata's request?
options: He left the decision to Sita
questions.explanation: Rama refused, insisting on honoring his father's promise and completing his exile.

questions.q: What did Bharata carry home in place of Rama himself?
questions.explanation: Rama gave Bharata his wooden sandals (paduka) to represent him on the throne.

questions.q: Why did Rama feel bound to remain in exile?
questions.explanation: Rama remained in exile out of duty to uphold the promise King Dasharatha had given.

questions.q: Whose word was Rama upholding by staying in the forest?
questions.explanation: Rama honored the vow his father, King Dasharatha, had made to Kaikeyi.

questions.q: What is the Sanskrit term for the wooden sandals Bharata took back?
questions.explanation: The wooden sandals are called paduka, which Bharata enshrined as a symbol of Rama's rule.

questions.q: How long was Rama bound to remain in exile?
questions.explanation: Rama's exile, as promised, lasted fourteen years, which he refused to cut short.

questions.q: After receiving the sandals, how did Bharata govern Ayodhya?
options: In the name of Rama's sandals
questions.explanation: Bharata ruled as a regent, placing Rama's sandals on the throne and acting in their name.

questions.q: Who was Bharata's mother, whose demand sent Rama into exile?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi, Bharata's mother, demanded Rama's exile and Bharata's coronation.

questions.q: What grievous event had occurred in Ayodhya before Bharata set out for Chitrakoot?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha had died of grief over Rama's exile, news Bharata brought to Chitrakoot.

questions.q: Where was Bharata when Rama was first sent into exile?
questions.explanation: Bharata was away at his maternal uncle's or grandfather's kingdom and had no part in the plot.

questions.q: How did Bharata react when he learned his mother had schemed for the throne on his behalf?
options: He was filled with horror and grief
questions.explanation: Bharata was horrified and condemned his mother's actions, refusing to benefit from them.

questions.q: Which brother accompanied Rama and Sita in exile and was present at Chitrakoot?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana faithfully accompanied Rama and Sita throughout the exile, including at Chitrakoot.

questions.q: When Lakshmana first saw Bharata's army approaching Chitrakoot, what did he initially suspect?
options: That Bharata came to honor Rama
That Bharata came to attack them
That it was a hunting party
questions.explanation: Lakshmana grew angry, suspecting Bharata had come with an army to harm Rama, until Rama calmed him.

questions.q: Who was the family priest and counselor who accompanied Bharata to persuade Rama?
questions.explanation: Sage Vasishtha, the royal preceptor, joined Bharata's party urging Rama to return.

questions.q: At whose hermitage did Bharata's large party rest on the way to Chitrakoot?
questions.explanation: Bharata's company was hosted at the ashram of Sage Bharadwaja near Prayaga on the journey.

questions.q: What did Bharata vow to do for the fourteen years until Rama's return?
options: Live as an ascetic outside the capital
Rule from the palace as king
Travel the world in search of Rama
questions.explanation: Bharata vowed to live austerely at Nandigrama, ruling only as Rama's deputy until his return.

questions.q: From which place outside Ayodhya did Bharata administer the kingdom in Rama's name?
questions.explanation: Bharata settled at Nandigrama, just outside Ayodhya, and governed in the name of the sandals.

questions.q: What lifestyle did Bharata adopt while waiting for Rama, in solidarity with his exiled brother?
options: An ascetic life of matted hair and bark garments
A life of seclusion in a cave
questions.explanation: Bharata wore bark and matted hair like an ascetic, refusing royal comforts during Rama's exile.

questions.q: What was the central reason Rama gave for why returning would be wrong?
options: He wanted Bharata to be king permanently
It would break his father's promise and dharma
questions.explanation: Rama held that abandoning the exile would violate dharma and dishonor his father's solemn word.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, which book (Kanda) contains Bharata's plea at Chitrakoot?
questions.explanation: The episode falls within the Ayodhya Kanda, which covers Rama's exile from the capital.

questions.q: Which brother was Bharata's devoted, inseparable companion who went with him to Chitrakoot?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna was Bharata's constant companion and accompanied him on the journey to plead with Rama.

questions.q: Which chieftain of the Nishadas warmly received Bharata's army on the banks of the Ganga?
questions.explanation: Guha, the Nishada king and Rama's friend, hosted Bharata and observed his sincerity.

questions.q: What did Rama place his foot upon to consecrate the sandals before giving them to Bharata?
questions.explanation: Rama stepped into the wooden sandals to sanctify them, then handed them to Bharata as his representatives.

questions.q: Which river flowed near Rama's hermitage at Chitrakoot?
questions.explanation: The Mandakini river flowed by Chitrakoot, where Rama dwelt during his early exile.

questions.q: How did Bharata honor the sandals once he returned to Ayodhya's domain?
options: He hung them at the city gate
He placed them on the royal throne under the white umbrella
questions.explanation: Bharata enthroned the sandals beneath the royal umbrella, offering them all the homage due a king.

questions.q: Whom did Bharata blame least and even defend, despite the injustice of the exile?
questions.explanation: Bharata defended and revered Rama, directing his condemnation at his mother's scheming rather than his blameless brother.

questions.q: What did Bharata declare he would do if Rama still refused to return after his plea?
options: Join Rama in the forest as an exile
questions.explanation: Bharata declared he would himself remain in the forest in exile if Rama would not come back, showing his devotion.
